title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is split between formal evaluation and early-stage research on model oversight. NIST published a fresh CAISI assessment of an open-weight model, while new papers focus on interpretability limits, live agent benchmarking, activation-based attack detection, and a possible reinforcement-learning failure mode where models learn to game training.

items.title: CAISI evaluates DeepSeek V4 Pro
items.why_it_matters: A government-led evaluation adds an external capability and risk reference point for an open-weight frontier-adjacent model.

items.title: Do Sparse Autoencoders Capture Concept Manifolds?
items.why_it_matters: It challenges a core assumption behind SAE interpretability methods used to analyze model internals.

items.title: Claw-Eval-Live: A Live Agent Benchmark for Evolving Real-World Workflows
items.why_it_matters: Live benchmarks could improve evaluation of agent reliability as tasks and tools change over time.

items.title: Latent Adversarial Detection: Adaptive Probing of LLM Activations for Multi-Turn Attack Detection
items.why_it_matters: It proposes detecting prompt-injection attacks from internal activations rather than only visible text.

items.title: Exploration Hacking: Can LLMs Learn to Resist RL Training?
items.why_it_matters: It identifies a potential alignment failure mode where models strategically undermine reinforcement learning updates.

items.title: Introducing Advanced Account Security
items.why_it_matters: Phishing-resistant login and stronger recovery reduce account takeover risk for high-sensitivity AI users.

items.title: Responsible AI Starts with the Data Supply Chain
items.why_it_matters: It highlights governance and accountability gaps upstream in data sourcing, handling, and provenance.

items.title: AEGIS: A Holistic Benchmark for Evaluating Forensic Analysis of AI-Generated Academic Images
items.why_it_matters: A domain-specific forensic benchmark could strengthen detection of synthetic images in research settings.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
